what_it_does: This file is a hub/index for the foundation/models folder. It catalogs local and cloud-routed models, organizes them by the jobs they fit, lists runtime/port status and disk/quant/architecture facts, records VRAM allocation patterns, and maintains an explicit register of open questions and untested gaps.
observations: Frontmatter marks it as a hub with type, title, folder, date_started, and tags.
Defines a one-file-per-model convention with kebab-case/underscore filenames derived from Hugging Face identifiers.
Uses a status taxonomy: deployed-default, swap-only, tested, downloaded, theoretical, auxiliary.

Organizes models under 'By need' use-cases such as high-throughput chat, long-context QA/RAG, tool calling, single-stream reasoning, dense/sparse/ColBERT embedding, code embedding, and multimodal (image, visual documents, audio).
Includes a Registry table with model, file link, disk size, quantization, architecture, status, runtime, and port.
Lists cloud routes accessible through the local Ollama endpoint with tier hints.
Summarizes three local runtimes: vLLM, Ollama, and jina-v4-env, including port and dependency notes.
Documents VRAM allocation on a 16 GiB RTX 4080, including a verified three-service co-resident default and swap procedures.
Ends with an 'Open at this folder' register naming known unknowns and pending characterizations.
Every performance/fit/status claim is dated, and the writing follows an 'examples-not-specs' open-future mode.
standouts: Strong operational specificity: ports, systemd services, swap commands, cold-start duration, and measured VRAM usage.
Explicit open-future discipline with dated claims, status taxonomy, and a visible backlog of untested items.
Models are framed around Tim-relevant jobs rather than generic AI capability categories.
Verified default stack of chat + embedding + multimodal embed running concurrently near full VRAM.
conventions: Wikilinks for hub-to-model references and cross-folder navigation.
Kebab-case/underscore filename mapping from Hugging Face identifiers.
Frontmatter with type, title, folder, date_started, tags.
Status taxonomy applied consistently across registry entries.
One file per model with a matching wikilink.
concerns: Several auxiliary models are present on disk but their actual integration into Tim's pipelines is undocumented.
Cloud route table lacks cost, latency, and rate-limit data, though this is acknowledged as open.
Some 'tested' figures come from single-session measurements and may not represent steady-state behavior.
The requested model value 'kimi-k2.7-code:cloud' does not appear in the file; only 'kimi-k2.5:cloud' and 'kimi-k2.6:cloud' are listed.
notes: A substrate-specific, dated model inventory that doubles as an operational dashboard and an extension guide, with explicit gaps rather than pretend completeness.
questions: What is the precise filename rule for identifiers with multiple namespace slashes or unusual version strings?
How are new 'By need' categories reviewed to prevent generic category creep?
What is the formal trigger for moving a model from downloaded to tested to deployed-default?
Were the VRAM and throughput figures measured under WSL or native Linux?
Is 'kimi-k2.7-code:cloud' intended to be added, or is it a mismatch with the listed kimi-k2.5/k2.6 cloud routes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a hub/index for the models folder, and the content matches that while also serving as a conventions guide and operational status board.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn a collection of downloaded weights and runtime configs into an inspectable, dated, gap-aware catalog that future agents can extend and Tim can use to pick the right model for a concrete job.
novelty: The tight integration of model catalog with runtime ports, VRAM budgets, swap commands, and an explicit 'Open' register, written in a personal-substrate voice rather than a generic model zoo.
contribution: A structured, dated, job-oriented index that records which models exist, how they are served, what operational constraints apply, and what remains unknown or untested.
summary_for_embedding: A hub index for the foundation/models folder cataloguing local and cloud-routed AI models by use-case need, runtime status, VRAM allocation, and open characterization questions.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an inspectable, dated, operational reference that helps agents choose and serve models on Tim's substrate while surfacing known gaps.
